channel.title: Computer Vision on Nemath Ahmed
channel.description: Recent content in Computer Vision on Nemath Ahmed

item.title: Achieving Photorealism With Neural Networks
item.description: Achieving Photorealism With Neural Networks Originally Published in TowardsDataScience Using synthetic data from GTA V In the research world, especially while dealing with images, there appears to be an increase of interest in using popular game environments, such as *Grand Theft Auto (GTA), *for experimentation. With today’s high-quality graphics, these environments produce synthetic images similar to real life, enabling researchers to test their approaches to intricate problems such as [this one](http://Domain Adaptation for Traffic Density Estimation), datasets for which are still nascent.

item.title: How do AI-Based fitness trackers work? Ft. 1D CNNs
item.description: An article on how the algorithms that come with AI-based fitness trackers for activity recognition work and a brief introduction to 1D-CNNs. Also published on Start it up. Art by me :) Fitness trackers are extremely à la mode these days. You see many people rocking one and it is indeed quite a novel piece of tech. But have you ever wondered how these devices work, be it a fitness wrist band recording all your sprints and strolls or a mobile phone counting your steps?
